full_prompt: Design a wearable flex-PCB biosensor patch housing (open-source medical-device brief) targeting continuous 3-lead ECG + reflectance PPG (pulse oximetry) monitoring over a 72-hour deployment on an adult chest. The patch is a single flexible polyimide (Kapton HN, E 2.5 GPa, tensile 231 MPa) PCB substrate encapsulated in a medical-grade silicone-gel over-mold (Dow MG 7-9800, Shore A 30, tensile 2.5 MPa, biocompat ISO 10993-10) with dimensions 80 mm x 35 mm x 4 mm total thickness, weighing no more than 8 g including a 30 mAh Li-polymer battery. Three dry Ag/AgCl electrodes contact skin in a triangular layout with 35 mm electrode-to-electrode pitch, and a single optical PPG window is located at the center. Pass/fail criteria: (R1) patch must flex over a 30 mm minimum skin radius of curvature without peel-off or silicone delamination, implying maximum substrate strain at 30 mm bend radius must remain below 2% (well within Kapton HN reversible strain), (R2) skin contact pressure through the silicone over-mold must not exceed 2 kPa at rest to enable 72-hour wear without pressure ulcer per ISO 10536-2 guidance, (R3) steady-state skin-side temperature increase from the patch electronics (100 mW average dissipation) at 30 C skin must not exceed 2 C to stay below IEC 60601-1 thermal comfort Clause 11.1.2.2 minus 2 C margin, (R4) patch must survive 100-cycle flex fatigue at 30 mm bend radius with no change in ECG signal amplitude greater than 5%, and (R5) total patch mass must not exceed 8 g and profile must not exceed 4 mm total thickness. Thermal analysis is required for R3. Vibration, fluid, and radiation analyses are not required. Deliver a STEP AP242 assembly with flex substrate, over-mold, electrodes, and battery as sub-bodies plus a verification report listing substrate strain at 30 mm bend, skin contact pressure, skin-side temperature rise, flex fatigue result, total mass, and total thickness.
short_prompt:
Design a wearable flex-PCB biosensor patch for continuous 3-lead ECG + reflectance PPG monitoring over 72 h on an adult chest. The patch is a flexible polyimide (Kapton HN) PCB with medical-grade silicone gel overmold (Dow MG 7-9800, Shore A 30), roughly 80 x 35 x 4 mm, 8 g max including a 30 mAh Li-polymer battery. Three dry Ag/AgCl electrodes are in a triangular layout with 35 mm pitch, with a central optical PPG window.

Design to IEC 60601-1 Clause 11.1.2.2 (skin-contact surface temperature) and ISO 10536-2-style pressure-ulcer prevention guidance. The patch back is adhered to skin via silicone; the front is free.

Load cases.
- LC1 bend: pure bend about the transverse axis at 30 mm radius.
- LC2 skin contact at rest (pressure distribution through the overmold).
- LC3 thermal steady state: 100 mW electronics dissipation against 30 C skin.
- LC4 flex fatigue: 100 bend cycles at 30 mm radius, check ECG amplitude change.

Total mass and total thickness are wearability constraints.

requires_non_fea_solver.reason: Pure-bend substrate strain at radius R is closed-form eps = t/2R, cross-checked by FEA.
examples: DuPont Kapton HN bend test data

prompt.title: Flex-PCB ECG + PPG wearable patch
prompt.brief: Design a 80 x 35 x 4 mm flexible polyimide PCB patch with silicone over-mold and 3 Ag/AgCl electrodes, passing 30 mm bend radius, 2 kPa skin pressure, 2 C skin-side temperature rise, 100-cycle flex fatigue, and 8 g mass.

material.substrate: Kapton HN polyimide, E 2.5 GPa, tensile 231 MPa

objective.primary: pass 72 h wearable comfort + signal integrity

recommended_fea_setup.element_type: shell for substrate, solid for over-mold

exclusions_explained.vibration_analysis: Wearable motion is handled as quasi-static bending.
exclusions_explained.fluid_analysis: Sweat moisture out of scope for this brief.
exclusions_explained.buckling_analysis: Membrane in bending, not compressive buckling.
